title: polyfil.io login attempt on quickviewing .md files
author: AdeleneDawner
body:
**Describe the bug**
Attempting to quickview an .md file results in a polyfill.io access attempt.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Quickview any .md file
2. Observe popup window requesting login credentials for polyfill.io over .md display window

**Expected behavior**
.md display window with no popup

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS Version: Windows 11 Version 10.0.26100 Build 26100
 - QuickLook Version: 3.7.3

title: Could add an auto-skip option for the non-native Office plugin when the native plugin is available?
author: ZUIcat
body:
Using QuickLook as a portable app across machines with/without Office. Native plugin provides superior quality, but non-native QuickLook.Plugin.OfficeViewer still intercepts previews when native is available. Both plugins are installed to ensure fallback (no Office) + best quality (with Office), but currently require manual toggling.

Add an option to the non-native plugin to auto-detect if native plugin can handle the file; if yes, skip and let native take over for better quality.

Manual enable/disable via settings or scripts – both inconvenient.

Portable QuickLook usage across varying environments; applies to non-native plugin only.

title: Video/Audio Playback Issue in QuickLook 4.x.x: WPFMediaKit Fail - Plus a Workaround!!!
author: bootlegseahorse
body: - OS Version: Windows 11 25H2 - QuickLook Version: All GitHub .exe or .msi installer versions from 4.0.0 to 4.5.0 Since QuickLook 4, I have been running into a very specific but serious issue: almost all video and audio files fail to play. They used to work perfectly in QuickLook 3. Sometimes QuickLook shows this error: `WPFMediaKit.WPFMediaKitException: Could not render any streams from the source Uri` Other times, it seems to load the audio file, but nothing happens even after clicking the play button. Because of this severe issue, I had stayed on version 3.7.2 for years. Recently, however, I finally found a workaround. The trick is to replace the "VideoViewer" plugin from QuickLook 4.x.x with the old plugin from QuickLook 3.7.2. Here are the steps: 1. Uninstall QuickLook 4.x.x. 2. Install QuickLook 3.7.2. 3. Go to your QuickLook installation folder, for example: `X:\...\QuickLook\` 4. Open the `QuickLook.Plugin` folder 5. Copy the folder named `QuickLook.Plugin.VideoViewer`, and save it somewhere else on your harddrive. 6. Install QuickLook 4.x.x again. 7. Go back to `X:\...\QuickLook\QuickLook.Plugin\` 8. Delete the existing `QuickLook.Plugin.VideoViewer` folder from the QuickLook 4 installation. 9. Paste the `QuickLook.Plugin.VideoViewer` folder that you copied from QuickLook 3.7.2. After doing this, restart QuickLook, and video and audio playback should work normally again. Hope this helps. Cheers!

title: Quick Look's window is unfocused with XYplorer
author: Skebops
body:
**Describe the bug**
QL's windows is unfocused when opened from XYplorer - i can't close the Quick look with space, i must click one more time to focus the window and then press the spacebar to close window

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open the XYplorer File manager
2. Open some image with Quick Look
3. Press the space bar to close Quick Look windows
4. Quick Look window is still present, space was typed to Xyplorer's "live filter" field

**Expected behavior**
Quick Look window should be focused after file was opened

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS Version:
Edition	Windows 11 Pro 25H2
OS build	26200.8655
 - QuickLook Version: QuickLook-4.5.0-177-gdccdc16.msi

**Screenshots and Exception Log**

title: Markdown changelog viewer shows popup referencing polyfill.io ("sign in to access this site")
author: ThereIsNoRealGod
body: After QuickLook auto-updated, the built-in changelog popup (rendered by the Markdown viewer) displayed an overlay reading something like "Sign in to access this site — AuthorizationRequired" attributed to https://polyfill.io. The overlay scrolled/moved along with the page content, indicating it was rendered inside the WebView2 context used by the Markdown viewer, not as a separate OS-level popup. polyfill.io is a domain that was acquired in 2024 and has since been used to inject malicious scripts (including phishing-style overlays) into any page that loads content from it (see: https://sansec.io/research/polyfill-supply-chain-attack). I suspect a JS dependency used by the Markdown rendering pipeline (possibly related to Mermaid or MathJax support added in recent versions) may reference this domain, directly or transitively. I did not click or interact with the overlay. To Reproduce Update QuickLook to the latest version The changelog automatically opens after update (rendered via Markdown viewer) Scroll/interact with the changelog page A "sign in" overlay appears, referencing polyfill.io Expected behavior The changelog should render with no external network calls beyond what's strictly needed (or none at all), and no unexpected sign-in prompts or overlays. Screenshots and Exception Log [tmpFF55.tmp.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29115653/tmpFF55.tmp.md)

title: [Feature Request] Support Sigma File Manager
author: roeseth
body:
Is this possible to support another 3rd party file manager? https://github.com/aleksey-hoffman/sigma-file-manager
Although sigma file manager has a built in quick view, it is not as extensible as quick look and I have been on quick look for so long that I don't want to move away.

Thank you!

title: Fix Dark Mode Bug
author: JohnChiang2026
body: *For all's convenience, please use English to fill this issue.* *Please search within existing issues before the submission. Has this issue already been reported?* **Describe the bug** A clear and concise description of what the bug is. When the QuickLook app is running, as we all know, if we click this app again in Start Menu, a windows will appear to remind us that this app is running. But recently, I found this window's style doesn't follow system's dark/light mode. It always stays in light mode. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Go to 'Start Menu' 2. Click on 'QuickLook'(make sure this app is running previously) 3. When the windows appears, we could see error. **Expected behavior** A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen. The window should follow system's dark/light mode to change its own style. **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS Version: Windows 11 25H2 - QuickLook Version: 4.5.5.0 from Microsoft Store **Screenshots and Exception Log** If applicable, add screenshots and logs to help explain your problem. The log file is located in [one of several locations](https://github.com/QL-Win/QuickLook/wiki/Differences-Between-Distributions#user-data-location).

title: System.ArgumentException: Illegal characters in path.
author: purbarunBC13
body:
QuickLook v4.5.0.0
6/7/2026 12:00:27 PM Windows 10.0.22631.2
```
System.ArgumentException: Illegal characters in path.
   at System.Security.Permissions.FileIOPermission.EmulateFileIOPermissionChecks(String fullPath)
   at System.Security.Permissions.FileIOPermission.QuickDemand(FileIOPermissionAccess access, String fullPath, Boolean checkForDuplicates, Boolean needFullPath)
   at System.IO.File.InternalGetLastWriteTimeUtc(String path, Boolean checkHost)
   at QuickLook.Plugin.InfoPanel.InfoPanel.DisplayInfo(String path)
   at QuickLook.Plugin.InfoPanel.Plugin.View(String path, ContextObject context)
   at QuickLook.ViewerWindow.<>c__DisplayClass7_0.<BeginShow>b__2()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at QuickLook.ViewerWindow.<>c__DisplayClass7_0.<BeginShow>b__2()
   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.InternalRealCall(Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs)
   at System.Windows.Threading.ExceptionWrapper.TryCatchWhen(Object source, Delegate callback, Object args, Int32 numArgs, Delegate catchHandler)
```

title: The Markdown viewer renders incorrectly after modifying the screen scaling factor.
author: MerrickZ
body: **Describe the bug** The Markdown viewer renders incorrectly after modifying the screen scaling factor. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Go to 'Windows11/Settings/System/Screen Scaling' 2. Click on '150%' 3. Run QuickLook 4. Preview some markdown file, works fine here. 5. Go to 'Windows11/Settings/System/Screen Scaling' 6. Change to other values like '125%' 7. Preview same markdown file, the display area is smaller than window area. **Expected behavior** webview display area should fit the window **Desktop (please complete the following information):** - OS Version: Windows 11 25H2 - QuickLook Version: MsStore 4.5.0 UWP **Screenshots and Exception Log** Should be: But: **Besides, Image viewer works fine**

title: QuickLook not automatically started after msi silent installation
author: phrossignol
body:
MSI installation command is as follows : 
msiexec /qn /norestart /i "QuickLook-4.5.0.msi" ALLUSERS=1
Installation is performing well but at the end QuickLook is not started.
In case of a non-silent installation QuickLook is started at the end of installation
-  
 - OS Version: Windows 11 25H2 (74 bits)
 - QuickLook Version: 4.5.0 from MSI installer]

title: Performance issue, about 500ms delay on v4.5.0/win11Canary
author: MerrickZ
body:
A significant ~500ms delay occurs when triggering the full preview (pressing the spacebar) in v4.5.0.

### Reproduction Steps

 - Select any file (image, video, text file — all tested)
 - Press the spacebar to open the preview window

### Expected Behavior (as in v3.4.0)

Preview window opens instantly with no perceptible delay when pressing spacebar.

### Actual Behavior (v4.5.0)

Consistent ~500ms lag between pressing spacebar and the preview window appearing (reproducible across all file types).

### System Info

```
Microsoft Windows 11 Pro Insider Preview 10.0.26300 Build 26300
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 154 Stepping 4 GenuineIntel ~1700 Mhz
```

title: [FR] Nightly build for EXE Setup (MicaSetup)
author: emako
body: Received feedback that the admin permission directory installed using the EXE installation package cannot be overwritten with the nightly MSI.

title: QuickLook.exe blocked by Windows 11 Smart App Control
author: Name12312
body: ### **Describe the bug:** When attempting to launch QuickLook, Windows 11 Smart App Control intervenes and completely blocks the execution of `QuickLook.exe`. The operating system throws a warning stating that the application may be unsafe because the publisher cannot be verified. This typically occurs when a code-signing certificate has expired, is missing entirely, or hasn't built enough reputation with Microsoft Defender SmartScreen. **Steps to reproduce the behavior:** 1. Ensure Smart App Control is enabled on a Windows 11 machine. 2. Download and install the standard desktop version of QuickLook. 3. Attempt to launch the application. 4. Observe the Smart App Control blocking notification. **Expected behavior** The application should launch normally and run in the background without triggering system-level security blocks. The executable needs a valid, recognized code-signing certificate to bypass Microsoft's Smart App Control. **Desktop Environment:** * OS: Windows 11 Pro (Version 25H2) * Installation: 4.5.0 (.msi)

title: Traditional Chinese (zh-TW) text not rendered in PPTX preview
author: naihongzhou1029
body:
*For all's convenience, this issue is filed in English.*

**Describe the bug**

When previewing a PowerPoint file (`.pptx`) that contains Traditional Chinese (zh-TW) text, QuickLook fails to render the CJK characters — slide titles, body paragraphs and table cells appear blank wherever Chinese should be. Latin characters, digits, punctuation, the table grid, and the slide background all render normally. The QuickLook window title bar shows the Chinese filename correctly, so the problem seems limited to the in-slide text renderer of the PPTX previewer.

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. On a Windows system with Traditional Chinese installed, prepare a `.pptx` that contains Traditional Chinese text (titles, paragraphs, and table cells).
2. In File Explorer, select the file and press <kbd>Space</kbd> to invoke QuickLook.
3. Observe the preview: every region that should contain Chinese characters is blank, while ASCII content (e.g. `115`, `EMRD`, `SEMI-EMRD`, `IB-101`, time ranges like `9:30-9:35`, `+`, `/`) is shown as expected.
4. Open the same file in PowerPoint / ONLYOFFICE on the same machine for comparison — all Chinese characters render correctly.

**Expected behavior**

All Traditional Chinese characters in the slide should be displayed, matching how the file is rendered by the source application (PowerPoint / ONLYOFFICE). The renderer should fall back to a system CJK font when the requested font does not cover the glyphs.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS Version: Windows 10 IoT Enterprise LTSC 2021 (10.0.19044)
 - System display language / locale: Traditional Chinese (zh-TW)
 - QuickLook Version: 4.5.0 (from MSI installer at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\QuickLook\QuickLook.exe`)
 - File type tested: `.pptx` (PowerPoint)

**Screenshots and Exception Log**

Screenshots will be attached in a follow-up comment / by editing this issue in the browser (the GitHub CLI cannot upload images directly):

1. QuickLook preview — Chinese text missing (only ASCII characters and layout visible). Filename in title bar `115_RD_SD畢業典禮流程及走位.pptx` renders correctly.
2. Reference — the same file opened in ONLYOFFICE, showing the full Traditional Chinese content (e.g. `115年EMRD暨SEMI-EMRD畢業典禮`, table headers `時間 / 主題 / 人物 / 備註`, row contents such as `典禮開始`, `請大家就座`, `長官致詞`, `院長/執行長/副執行長`, etc.).

**Additional notes**

The file opens correctly in PowerPoint and ONLYOFFICE on the same machine, so the necessary CJK fonts are installed system-wide. This strongly suggests the QuickLook PPTX plugin is not falling back to a CJK-capable font when the embedded/requested font does not cover the glyphs, rather than a font-availability issue.

title: It is hoped that the MSI installation method supports overwriting update installation.
author: NeatFreedom
body:
It is hoped that the MSI installation method supports overwriting update installation.
Currently, the MSI installation method requires uninstalling the previous version before reinstalling, which is quite troublesome.
